addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1 # General Information 2 3 The *en_cl_fix* library allows implementing the same algorithms and calculations in different languages (currently VHDL and MATLAB) easily. The main idea behind this is to evaluate the behavior or an algorithm in a high-level language such as MATLAB while implementing it later in VHDL. Thanks to the bit-true models, the correctness of the VHDL implementation can be checked easily by comparing outputs of both implementations 4 5 ## License 6 This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development. 7 8 ## Maintainers 9 Martin Heimlicher [martin.heimlicher@enclustra.com] 10 11 ## Changelog 12 See [Changelog](Changelog.md) 13 14 ## Detailed Documentation 15 See [VHDL Documentation](https://rawgit.com/enclustra/en_cl_fix/master/doc/vhdl_out/index.html) 16 17 ## Tagging Policy 18 Stable releases are tagged in the form *major*.*minor*.*bugfix*. 19 20 * Whenever a change is not fully backward compatible, the *major* version number is incremented 21 * Whenever new features are added, the *minor* version number is incremented 22 * If only bugs are fixed (i.e. no functional changes are applied), the *bugfix* version is incremented 23 24 # Dependencies 25 26 The python implementation depends on the *numpy* package 27 28 # Simulations and Testbenches 29 30 * Python Models 31 * Navigate to *python/unittest* 32 * Run *python3 en_cl_fix_pkg_test.py* 33 * VHDL Models 34 * Start Mmodelsim 35 * In the TCL console, navigate to *sim* 36 * run *source ./sim.tcl* 37 * The script automatically prints whether all tests were successful 38 * MATLAB 39 * Currently there are not tests for the MATLAB implementation 40 41 # Fixed Point Number Format 42 43 ## Format 44 45 The fixed point number format used in this library is defined as follows: 46 47 [s, i, f] 48 49 s: true = Signed number (two's complement), false = Unsigned number 50 i: Number of integer bits 51 f: Number of fractional bits 52 53 The total number of bits required is s+i+f. 54 55 The value of each bit depending on its position relative to the binary point (i-bits left, f-bits right) is given below. 56 57 ... [4][2][1]**.**[0.5][0.25][0.125] ... 58 59 Some examples are given below: 60 61 | Number Format | Range | Bit Pattern | Example Int | Example Bits | 62 |:-------------:|:-----------------:|:------------:|:-----------:|:------------:| 63 | [true,2,1] | -4 ... +3.5 | sii.f | -2.5 | 101.1 | 64 | [true,2,2] | -4 ... +3.75 | sii.ff | -2.5 | 101.10 | 65 | [false,4,0] | 0 ... 15 | iiii. | 5 | 0101. | 66 | [false,4,2] | 0 ... 15.75 | iiii.ff | 5.25 | 0101.01 | 67 | [true,4,-2] | -16 ... 12 | sii--. | -8 | 110--. | 68 | [true,-2,4] | -0.25 ... +0.1875 | s.--ff | 0.125 | 0.--10 | 69 70 ## Rounding 71 72 Several rounding modes are implemented. They are described below 73 74 75 76 77 78 79 80 81 82 83 84 85 86 87 88 89 90 91 92 93 94 95 96 97 98 99 100 101 102 103 104 105 106 107 108 109 110 111 112 113 114 115 116 117
Value Description Examples rounded to (true,2,0)
2.2 2.7 -1.5 -0.5 0.5 1.5
Trunc_s Cut off bits without any rounding 2 2 -2 -1 0 1
NonSymPos_s Non-symmetric rounding to positive 2 3 -1 0 1 2
NonSymNeg_s Non-symmetric rounding to negative 2 3 -2 -1 0 1
SymInf_s Symmetric rounding to infinity 2 3 -2 -1 1 2
SymZero_s Symmetric rounding to zero 2 3 -1 0 0 1
ConvEven_s Convergent rounding to even numbers 2 3 -2 0 0 2
ConvOdd_s Convertent rounding to odd numbers 2 3 -1 -1 1 1
118 119 *NonSymPos_s* is the most common rounding mode and often aliased as *Round_s* for simplicity and readability. 120 121 **NOTE:** Use *Trunc_s* wherever possible for lowest resource usage. If rounding is required, prefer *NonSymPos_s* for low resource usage. 122 123 124 # Documentation 125 126 Documentation for each implementation is written in an appropriate way for the corresponding languages. 127 128 * VHDL - Doxygen 129 * To re-generate the documentation, install doxygen, open the doxygen project *doc/doxy_vhdl.doxy* and run doxygen. 130 * MATLAB - Documentation Comments 131 * The comments are displayed by matlab automatically when typing *help * 132 * Prerequisite is to add the path to the .m files to the MATLAB path 133 134 135 136

# General Information The *en_cl_fix* library allows implementing the same algorithms and calculations in different languages (currently VHDL and MATLAB) easily. The main idea behind this is to evaluate the behavior or an algorithm in a high-level language such as MATLAB while implementing it later in VHDL. Thanks to the bit-true models, the correctness of the VHDL implementation can be checked easily by comparing outputs of both implementations ## License This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development. ## Maintainers Martin Heimlicher [martin.heimlicher@enclustra.com] ## Changelog See [Changelog](Changelog.md) ## Detailed Documentation See [VHDL Documentation](https://rawgit.com/enclustra/en_cl_fix/master/doc/vhdl_out/index.html) ## Tagging Policy Stable releases are tagged in the form *major*.*minor*.*bugfix*. * Whenever a change is not fully backward compatible, the *major* version number is incremented * Whenever new features are added, the *minor* version number is incremented * If only bugs are fixed (i.e. no functional changes are applied), the *bugfix* version is incremented # Dependencies The python implementation depends on the *numpy* package # Simulations and Testbenches * Python Models * Navigate to *python/unittest* * Run *python3 en_cl_fix_pkg_test.py* * VHDL Models * Start Mmodelsim * In the TCL console, navigate to *sim* * run *source ./sim.tcl* * The script automatically prints whether all tests were successful * MATLAB * Currently there are not tests for the MATLAB implementation # Fixed Point Number Format ## Format The fixed point number format used in this library is defined as follows: [s, i, f] s: true = Signed number (two's complement), false = Unsigned number i: Number of integer bits f: Number of fractional bits The total number of bits required is s+i+f. The value of each bit depending on its position relative to the binary point (i-bits left, f-bits right) is given below. ... [4][2][1]**.**[0.5][0.25][0.125] ... Some examples are given below: | Number Format | Range | Bit Pattern | Example Int | Example Bits | |:-------------:|:-----------------:|:------------:|:-----------:|:------------:| | [true,2,1] | -4 ... +3.5 | sii.f | -2.5 | 101.1 | | [true,2,2] | -4 ... +3.75 | sii.ff | -2.5 | 101.10 | | [false,4,0] | 0 ... 15 | iiii. | 5 | 0101. | | [false,4,2] | 0 ... 15.75 | iiii.ff | 5.25 | 0101.01 | | [true,4,-2] | -16 ... 12 | sii--. | -8 | 110--. | | [true,-2,4] | -0.25 ... +0.1875 | s.--ff | 0.125 | 0.--10 | ## Rounding Several rounding modes are implemented. They are described below
Value Description Examples rounded to (true,2,0)
2.2 2.7 -1.5 -0.5 0.5 1.5
Trunc_s Cut off bits without any rounding 2 2 -2 -1 0 1
NonSymPos_s Non-symmetric rounding to positive 2 3 -1 0 1 2
NonSymNeg_s Non-symmetric rounding to negative 2 3 -2 -1 0 1
SymInf_s Symmetric rounding to infinity 2 3 -2 -1 1 2
SymZero_s Symmetric rounding to zero 2 3 -1 0 0 1
ConvEven_s Convergent rounding to even numbers 2 3 -2 0 0 2
ConvOdd_s Convertent rounding to odd numbers 2 3 -1 -1 1 1
*NonSymPos_s* is the most common rounding mode and often aliased as *Round_s* for simplicity and readability. **NOTE:** Use *Trunc_s* wherever possible for lowest resource usage. If rounding is required, prefer *NonSymPos_s* for low resource usage. # Documentation Documentation for each implementation is written in an appropriate way for the corresponding languages. * VHDL - Doxygen * To re-generate the documentation, install doxygen, open the doxygen project *doc/doxy_vhdl.doxy* and run doxygen. * MATLAB - Documentation Comments * The comments are displayed by matlab automatically when typing *help * * Prerequisite is to add the path to the .m files to the MATLAB path

## 1.2.0
* Features
  * Added wide fixed-point (> 53 bits) Python support
* Bugfixes
  * Added workaround for Xilinx Vivado bug (resolution of "mod" operator)
  * Fixed non-compliant VHDL string indexes
  * Fixed cl_fix_resize crashes when DropFracBits_c >= a'length
  * Fixed case-sensitivity bug in string_parse_boolean (VHDL)
  * Fixed inconsistency between cl_fix_in_range and cl_fix_resize (VHDL)

## 1.1.8

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed and cleaned up support for large numbers (>31 bits)
  * Fixed synthesis issues with add/sub

## 1.1.7

  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed a bug in the testbench

## 1.1.6

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed "numbers > 31 bits for cl\_fix\_from\_real" for Modelsim

## 1.1.5

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Support numbers > 31 bits for cl\_fix\_from\_real
    * In this case the result is not exact (only upper 31 bits are correct) and a warning is printed

## 1.1.4

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed bug in cl\_fix\_from\_real (saturation did not work)

## 1.1.3

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Remove VHDL-2008 statements to make the code runnable in Vivado Simulator

## 1.1.2

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed bug in cl\_fix\_max\_real that led to problems with GHDL

## 1.1.1

* Features
  * None
* Bugfixes
  * Fixed bug in cl\_fix\_from\_real that led to integer overflows for the format (false,0,31)

## 1.1.0

* Features
  * Added Python Implementation incl. Unit-Test
  * Added Testbench for VHDL Implementation
* Bugfixes
  * None

## 1.0.0

* First Release containing VHDL and MATLAB implementations

content.text: 这是一个定点数库。让我阅读一下这三个实现中的核心源代码文件。

content.content:
1	########################################################################################################################
2	#  Copyright (c) 2018 by Paul Scherrer Institute, Switzerland
3	#  All rights reserved.
4	#  Authors: Oliver Bruendler
5	########################################################################################################################
6	
7	########################################################################################################################
8	# Imports
9	########################################################################################################################
10	import numpy as np
11	import warnings
12	import random
13	
14	from .en_cl_fix_types import *
15	from .wide_fxp import wide_fxp
16	
17	########################################################################################################################
18	# Bittrue available in VHDL
19	########################################################################################################################
20	def cl_fix_width(fmt : FixFormat) -> int:
21	    return fmt.width()
22	
23	def cl_fix_is_wide(fmt : FixFormat) -> bool:
24	    # Determine whether "narrow" (double precision float) or "wide" (arbitrary-precision integer)
25	    # fixed-point representation should be used for this fixed-point format.
26	    # An IEEE 754 double has:
27	    #   * 1 explicit sign bit.
28	    #   * 11 exponent bits (supports -1022 to +1023 due to values reserved for special cases).
29	    #   * 52 fractional bits.
30	    #   * 1 implicit integer bit := '1'.
31	    # The values +0 and -0 are supported as special cases (exponent = 0x000). This means integers
32	    # on [-2**53, 2**53] can be represented exactly. In other words, if we assume the exponent
33	    # is never overflowed, then 54-bit signed numbers and 53-bit unsigned numbers are guaranteed to
34	    # be represented exactly. In theory, this would mean: return fmt.IntBits + fmt.FracBits > 53.
35	    # However, handling wrapping of signed numbers (when saturation is disabled) is made simpler if
36	    # we reserve an extra integer bit for signed numbers. This gives a consistent 53-bit limit for
37	    # both signed and unsigned numbers.
38	    return cl_fix_width(fmt) > 53
39	
40	def cl_fix_string_from_format(fmt : FixFormat) -> str:
41	    return str(fmt)
42	
43	def cl_fix_max_value(rFmt : FixFormat):
44	    if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
45	        return wide_fxp.MaxValue(rFmt)
46	    else:
47	        return 2.0**rFmt.IntBits-2.0**(-rFmt.FracBits)
48	
49	def cl_fix_min_value(rFmt : FixFormat):
50	    if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
51	        return wide_fxp.MinValue(rFmt)
52	    else:
53	        if rFmt.Signed:
54	            return -2.0**rFmt.IntBits
55	        else:
56	            return 0.0
57	
58	def cl_fix_sign(a, aFmt : FixFormat):
59	    if not aFmt.Signed:
60	        return 0
61	    else:
62	        return np.where(a < 0, 1, 0)
63	
64	def cl_fix_int(a, aFmt : FixFormat):
65	    # Extract the integer part of the data values.
66	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
67	        r = a.floor()
68	        # Handle truncation to narrow representation.
69	        if cl_fix_is_wide(r.fmt):
70	            return r
71	        else:
72	            return r.to_narrow_fxp()
73	    else:
74	        rFmt = FixFormat(aFmt.Signed, aFmt.IntBits, 0)
75	        r = np.floor(a)
76	        # Handle expansion to wide_fxp (FracBits < 0).
77	        if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
78	            return wide_fxp.FromNarrowFxp(r, rFmt)
79	        else:
80	            return r
81	
82	def cl_fix_frac(a, aFmt : FixFormat):
83	    # Extract the fractional part of the data values.
84	    # Note: Result has implicit frac bits if IntBits<0.
85	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
86	        r = a.frac_part()
87	        
88	        # Handle truncation to narrow representation.
89	        if cl_fix_is_wide(r.fmt):
90	            return r
91	        else:
92	            return r.to_narrow_fxp()
93	    else:
94	        if aFmt.Signed and aFmt.FracBits > 53:
95	            warnings.warn("cl_fix_frac : Possible loss of precision. Consider using wide_fxp.", Warning)
96	        
97	        # Drop the sign bit
98	        if aFmt.Signed:
99	            offset = 2**aFmt.IntBits
100	            a = np.where(a < 0, a + offset, a)
101	        
102	        # Retain fractional LSBs
103	        return a % 2**min(aFmt.IntBits, 0)
104	        
105	def cl_fix_combine(sign : int, intbits : int, fracbits : int, rFmt : FixFormat):
106	    # Combines separate {sign_bit, integer_part, fractional_part} into a fixed-point number.
107	    # For example: combine(0, 5, 1, FixFormat(True, 4, 2)) <==> 5.25
108	    if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
109	        val = -sign*2**(rFmt.IntBits+rFmt.FracBits) + intbits*2**rFmt.FracBits + fracbits
110	        return wide_fxp(val, rFmt)
111	    else:
112	        return -sign*2.0**rFmt.IntBits + intbits + fracbits*2.0**-rFmt.FracBits
113	
114	def cl_fix_get_msb(a, aFmt : FixFormat, index : int):
115	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
116	        return a.get_msb(index)
117	    else:
118	        a = np.array(a)
119	        if aFmt.Signed:
120	            if index == 0:
121	                return (a < 0).astype(int)
122	            else:
123	                return ((a * 2.0 ** (index - aFmt.IntBits - 1)) % 1 >= 0.5).astype(int)
124	        else:
125	            return ((a * 2.0 ** (index - aFmt.IntBits)) % 1 >= 0.5).astype(int)
126	
127	def cl_fix_get_lsb(a, aFmt : FixFormat, index : int):
128	    return cl_fix_get_msb(a, aFmt, cl_fix_width(aFmt)-1-index)
129	
130	def cl_fix_set_msb(a, aFmt : FixFormat, index : int, value):
131	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
132	        return a.set_msb(index, value)
133	    else:
134	        if np.any(value > 1) or np.any(value < 0):
135	            raise Exception("cl_fix_set_msb: only 1 and 0 allowed for value")
136	        value = int(value)
137	        current = cl_fix_get_msb(a, aFmt, index)
138	        if aFmt.Signed:
139	            if index == 0:
140	                return ((value - 0.5) - (current - 0.5)) * -2.0 ** (aFmt.IntBits) + a
141	            else:
142	                return ((value - 0.5) - (current - 0.5)) * 2.0 ** (aFmt.IntBits - index) + a
143	        else:
144	            return ((value - 0.5) - (current - 0.5)) * 2.0 ** (aFmt.IntBits - index - 1) + a
145	
146	def cl_fix_set_lsb(a, aFmt : FixFormat, index : int, value):
147	    return cl_fix_set_msb(a, aFmt, cl_fix_width(aFmt)-1-index, value)
148	
149	def cl_fix_from_real(a, rFmt : FixFormat, saturate : FixSaturate = FixSaturate.SatWarn_s):
150	    
151	    if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
152	        return wide_fxp.FromFloat(a, rFmt, saturate)
153	    else:
154	        # Saturation warning
155	        if (saturate == FixSaturate.SatWarn_s) or (saturate == FixSaturate.Warn_s):
156	            amax = np.max(a)
157	            amin = np.min(a)
158	            if amax > cl_fix_max_value(rFmt):
159	                warnings.warn(f"cl_fix_from_real: Number {amax} exceeds maximum for format {rFmt}", Warning)
160	            if amin < cl_fix_min_value(rFmt):
161	                warnings.warn(f"cl_fix_from_real: Number {amin} exceeds minimum for format {rFmt}", Warning)
162	        
163	        # Quantize. Always use half-up rounding.
164	        x = np.floor(a*(2.0**rFmt.FracBits)+0.5)/2.0**rFmt.FracBits
165	        
166	        # Saturate
167	        if (saturate == FixSaturate.Sat_s) or (saturate == FixSaturate.SatWarn_s):
168	            x = np.where(x > cl_fix_max_value(rFmt), cl_fix_max_value(rFmt), x)
169	            x = np.where(x < cl_fix_min_value(rFmt), cl_fix_min_value(rFmt), x)
170	        
171	        return x
172	
173	def cl_fix_from_bits_as_int(a : int, aFmt : FixFormat):
174	    if cl_fix_is_wide(aFmt):
175	        if not np.all(cl_fix_in_range(a, aFmt, aFmt)):
176	            raise ValueError("cl_fix_from_bits_as_int: Value not in number format range")
177	        return wide_fxp(a, aFmt)
178	    else:
179	        value = np.array(a/2**aFmt.FracBits, np.float64)
180	        if not np.all(cl_fix_in_range(value, aFmt, aFmt)):
181	            raise ValueError("cl_fix_from_bits_as_int: Value not in number format range")
182	        return value
183	
184	def cl_fix_get_bits_as_int(a, aFmt : FixFormat):
185	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
186	        return a.data
187	    else:
188	        return np.array(np.round(a*2.0**aFmt.FracBits),'int64')
189	
190	def cl_fix_resize(  a, aFmt : FixFormat,
191	                    rFmt : FixFormat,
192	                    rnd : FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat : FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
193	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
194	        # Convert to wide_fxp (if not already wide_fxp)
195	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
196	        # Resize
197	        result = a.resize(rFmt, rnd, sat)
198	        # Convert to narrow if required
199	        if not cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
200	            result = result.to_narrow_fxp()
201	    else:
202	        a = np.array(a)
203	        # Rounding
204	        bitGrowth = 0
205	        if rFmt.FracBits < aFmt.FracBits:
206	            if rnd is FixRound.Trunc_s:
207	                # No offset is applied, so no bit-growth
208	                bitGrowth = 0
209	            elif rnd is FixRound.NonSymPos_s:
210	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1)
211	                bitGrowth = 1
212	            elif rnd is FixRound.NonSymNeg_s:
213	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1) - 2.0 ** -aFmt.FracBits
214	                bitGrowth = 1
215	            elif rnd is FixRound.SymInf_s:
216	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1) - 2.0 ** -aFmt.FracBits * (a < 0).astype(int)
217	                bitGrowth = 1
218	            elif rnd is FixRound.SymZero_s:
219	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1) - 2.0 ** -aFmt.FracBits * (a >= 0).astype(int)
220	                bitGrowth = 1
221	            elif rnd is FixRound.ConvEven_s:
222	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1) - 2.0 ** -aFmt.FracBits * ((np.floor(a * 2 ** rFmt.FracBits) + 1) % 2)
223	                bitGrowth = 1
224	            elif rnd is FixRound.ConvOdd_s:
225	                a = a + 2.0 ** (-rFmt.FracBits - 1) - 2.0 ** -aFmt.FracBits * ((np.floor(a * 2 ** rFmt.FracBits)) % 2)
226	                bitGrowth = 1
227	            else:
228	                raise Exception("cl_fix_resize : Illegal value for round!")
229	        # The format after rounding is the same as after a (lossy) right shift by (aFmt.FracBits - rFmt.FracBits), but
230	        # with +bitGrowth (integer bit) to support the rounding offset applied above.
231	        roundedFmt = FixFormat(aFmt.Signed, aFmt.IntBits - (aFmt.FracBits - rFmt.FracBits) + bitGrowth, rFmt.FracBits)
232	        rounded = np.floor(a * 2.0 ** rFmt.FracBits).astype(float) * 2.0 ** -rFmt.FracBits
233	
234	        # Saturation warning
235	        fmtMax = cl_fix_max_value(rFmt)
236	        fmtMin = cl_fix_min_value(rFmt)
237	        if sat == FixSaturate.Warn_s or sat == FixSaturate.SatWarn_s:
238	            if np.any(rounded > fmtMax) or np.any(rounded < fmtMin):
239	                warnings.warn("cl_fix_resize : Saturation warning!", Warning)
240	
241	        # Saturation
242	        if sat == FixSaturate.None_s or sat == FixSaturate.Warn_s:
243	            # Wrap
244	            
245	            # Decide if signed wrapping calculation will fit in narrow format
246	            if rFmt.Signed:
247	                # We need to add: rounded + 2.0 ** rFmt.IntBits
248	                offsetFmt = FixFormat(0,rFmt.IntBits+1,0)  # Format of 2.0 ** rFmt.IntBits.
249	                addFmt = FixFormat.ForAdd(roundedFmt, offsetFmt)
250	                convertToWide = cl_fix_is_wide(addFmt)
251	            else:
252	                convertToWide = False
253	            
254	            if convertToWide:
255	                # Do intermediate calculation in wide_fxp (int) to avoid loss of precision
256	                rounded = np.floor(rounded.astype(object) * 2**rFmt.FracBits)
257	                satSpan = 2**(rFmt.IntBits + rFmt.FracBits)
258	                if rFmt.Signed:
259	                    result = ((rounded + satSpan) % (2*satSpan)) - satSpan
260	                else:
261	                    result = rounded % satSpan
262	                # Convert back to narrow fixed-point
263	                result = (result / 2**rFmt.FracBits).astype(float)
264	            else:
265	                # Calculate in float64 without loss of precision
266	                if rFmt.Signed:
267	                    result = ((rounded + 2.0 ** rFmt.IntBits) % (2.0 ** (rFmt.IntBits + 1))) - 2.0 ** rFmt.IntBits
268	                else:
269	                    result = rounded % (2.0**rFmt.IntBits)
270	        else:
271	            # Saturate
272	            result = np.where(rounded > fmtMax, fmtMax, rounded)
273	            result = np.where(rounded < fmtMin, fmtMin, result)
274	
275	    return result
276	
277	def cl_fix_in_range(    a, aFmt : FixFormat,
278	                        rFmt : FixFormat,
279	                        rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s):
280	    rndFmt = FixFormat(aFmt.Signed, aFmt.IntBits+1, rFmt.FracBits)
281	    valRnd = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, rndFmt, rnd, FixSaturate.Sat_s)
282	    lo = np.where(valRnd < cl_fix_min_value(rFmt), False, True)
283	    hi = np.where(valRnd > cl_fix_max_value(rFmt), False, True)
284	    return np.where(np.logical_and(lo,hi), True, False)
285	
286	def cl_fix_abs( a, aFmt : FixFormat,
287	                rFmt : FixFormat,
288	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
289	    midFmt = FixFormat.ForNeg(aFmt)
290	    aNeg = cl_fix_neg(a, aFmt, midFmt)
291	    aPos = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, midFmt)
292	    
293	    a = np.where(a < 0, aNeg, aPos)
294	    return cl_fix_resize(a, midFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
295	
296	def cl_fix_sabs(a, aFmt : FixFormat,
297	                rFmt : FixFormat,
298	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
299	    return cl_fix_sneg(a, aFmt, a < 0, rFmt, rnd, sat)
300	
301	def cl_fix_neg(a, aFmt : FixFormat,
302	              rFmt : FixFormat,
303	              rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
304	    midFmt = FixFormat.ForNeg(aFmt)
305	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or cl_fix_is_wide(midFmt):
306	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
307	    else:
308	        a = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, midFmt)
309	    
310	    return cl_fix_resize(-a, midFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
311	
312	def cl_fix_sneg(a, aFmt : FixFormat,
313	                enable : bool,
314	                rFmt : FixFormat,
315	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
316	    enable = np.array(enable)
317	    temp_fmt = FixFormat(True, aFmt.IntBits, max(aFmt.FracBits, rFmt.FracBits))
318	    temp = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, temp_fmt, FixRound.Trunc_s, FixSaturate.None_s)
319	    if type(temp) == wide_fxp:
320	        temp = -(enable.astype(int)) + (-1) ** enable.astype(int)*temp.data
321	        temp = wide_fxp(temp, temp_fmt)
322	    else:
323	        temp = -(enable.astype(int))*2 ** -temp_fmt.FracBits + (-1.0) ** enable.astype(int)*temp
324	        temp = temp.astype(float)
325	    return cl_fix_resize(temp, temp_fmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
326	
327	def cl_fix_add( a, aFmt : FixFormat,
328	                b, bFmt : FixFormat,
329	                rFmt : FixFormat,
330	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
331	    midFmt = FixFormat.ForAdd(aFmt, bFmt)
332	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or type(b) == wide_fxp or cl_fix_is_wide(midFmt):
333	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
334	        b = wide_fxp.FromFxp(b, bFmt)
335	    else:
336	        a = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, midFmt)
337	        b = cl_fix_resize(b, bFmt, midFmt)
338	    
339	    return cl_fix_resize(a + b, midFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
340	
341	def cl_fix_sub( a, aFmt : FixFormat,
342	                b, bFmt : FixFormat,
343	                rFmt : FixFormat,
344	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
345	    midFmt = FixFormat.ForSub(aFmt, bFmt)
346	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or type(b) == wide_fxp or cl_fix_is_wide(midFmt):
347	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
348	        b = wide_fxp.FromFxp(b, bFmt)
349	    else:
350	        a = cl_fix_resize(a, aFmt, midFmt)
351	        b = cl_fix_resize(b, bFmt, midFmt)
352	    
353	    return cl_fix_resize(a - b, midFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
354	
355	def cl_fix_addsub(  a, aFmt : FixFormat,
356	                    b, bFmt : FixFormat,
357	                    add,    #bool or bool array
358	                    rFmt : FixFormat,
359	                    rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
360	    radd = cl_fix_add(a, aFmt, b, bFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
361	    rsub = cl_fix_sub(a, aFmt, b, bFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
362	    return np.where(add, radd, rsub)
363	
364	def cl_fix_saddsub( a, aFmt : FixFormat,
365	                    b, bFmt : FixFormat,
366	                    add,    #bool or bool array
367	                    rFmt : FixFormat,
368	                    rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
369	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or type(b) == wide_fxp:
370	        # TODO
371	        raise NotImplementedError()
372	    else:
373	        temp_fmt = FixFormat.ForAdd(aFmt, bFmt)
374	        notAdd = np.array(np.logical_not(add),dtype="int32")
375	        temp = a + (-1.0) ** notAdd * b - notAdd * 2.0 ** -temp_fmt.FracBits
376	        return cl_fix_resize(temp, temp_fmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
377	
378	def cl_fix_mean(a, aFmt : FixFormat,
379	                b, bFmt : FixFormat,
380	                rFmt : FixFormat,
381	                rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
382	    temp_fmt = FixFormat.ForAdd(aFmt, bFmt)
383	    temp = cl_fix_add(a, aFmt, b, bFmt, temp_fmt, FixRound.Trunc_s, FixSaturate.None_s)
384	    return cl_fix_shift(temp, temp_fmt, -1, rFmt, rnd, sat)
385	
386	def cl_fix_mean_angle(  a, aFmt : FixFormat,
387	                        b, bFmt : FixFormat,
388	                        precise : bool,
389	                        rFmt : FixFormat,
390	                        rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
391	    raise NotImplementedError()
392	
393	def cl_fix_shift(  a, aFmt : FixFormat,
394	                   shift : int,
395	                   rFmt : FixFormat,
396	                   rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
397	    # Note: This function performs a lossless shift (equivalent to *2.0**shift), then resizes to
398	    #       the output format. The initial shift does NOT truncate any bits.
399	    # Note: "shift" direction is left. (So shift<0 shifts right).
400	    if cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
401	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
402	    if type(a) == wide_fxp:
403	        if np.ndim(shift) == 0:
404	            # Constant shift
405	            temp_fmt = FixFormat.ForShift(aFmt, shift)
406	            # Change format without changing data values => shift
407	            tmp = wide_fxp(a.data, temp_fmt)
408	            return cl_fix_resize(tmp, temp_fmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
409	        else:
410	            # Variable shift (each value individually)
411	            assert np.ndim(shift) == 1, "cl_fix_shift : shift must be 0d or 1d"
412	            assert shift.size == a.data.size, "cl_fix_shift : shift must be 0d or the same length as a"
413	            r = wide_fxp(np.zeros(a.data.size, dtype=object), rFmt)
414	            for i, s in enumerate(shift):
415	                temp_fmt = FixFormat.ForShift(aFmt, s)
416	                # Change format without changing data values => shift
417	                tmp = wide_fxp._FromIntScalar(a.data[i], temp_fmt)
418	                # Resize to rFmt
419	                r._data[i] = tmp.resize(rFmt, rnd, sat).data[0]
420	            
421	            # Convert to narrow if required
422	            if not cl_fix_is_wide(rFmt):
423	                r = r.to_narrow_fxp()
424	            
425	            return r
426	    else:
427	        temp_fmt = FixFormat.ForShift(aFmt, np.min(shift), np.max(shift))
428	        return cl_fix_resize(a * 2.0 ** shift, temp_fmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
429	
430	def cl_fix_mult(    a, aFmt : FixFormat,
431	                    b, bFmt : FixFormat,
432	                    rFmt : FixFormat,
433	                    rnd: FixRound = FixRound.Trunc_s, sat: FixSaturate = FixSaturate.None_s):
434	    midFmt = FixFormat.ForMult(aFmt, bFmt)
435	    if type(a) == wide_fxp or type(b) == wide_fxp or cl_fix_is_wide(midFmt):
436	        a = wide_fxp.FromFxp(a, aFmt)
437	        b = wide_fxp.FromFxp(b, bFmt)
438	    
439	    return cl_fix_resize(a * b, midFmt, rFmt, rnd, sat)
440	
441	########################################################################################################################
442	# File I/O
443	########################################################################################################################
444	
445	def cl_fix_write_formats(fmts, names, filename):
446	
447	    with open(filename, "w") as fid:
448	        # Write header
449	        header = "# " + ",".join(names)
450	        fid.write(header + "\n")
451	        
452	        # Allow input to be a scalar fmt
453	        if np.ndim(fmts) == 0:
454	            fmts = np.array(fmts, ndmin=1)
455	        
456	        for fmt in fmts:
457	            fid.write(cl_fix_string_from_format(fmt) + "\n")
458	    
459	########################################################################################################################
460	# Simulation utility functions (not available in VHDL)
461	########################################################################################################################
462	
463	def cl_fix_random(n : int, fmt : FixFormat):
464	    # Generate n random data values, distributed across the whole dynamic range of fmt.
465	    fmtLo = cl_fix_min_value(fmt)
466	    fmtHi = cl_fix_max_value(fmt)
467	    if cl_fix_is_wide(fmt):
468	        xint = np.empty((n,), dtype=object)
469	        for i in range(n):
470	            xint[i] = random.randrange(fmtLo.data, fmtHi.data+1)
471	
472	        return wide_fxp(xint, fmt)
473	    else:
474	        intLo = fmtLo*2**fmt.FracBits
475	        intHi = fmtHi*2**fmt.FracBits
476	        xint = np.random.randint(intLo, intHi+1, (n,), 'int64')
477	        return (xint / 2**fmt.FracBits).astype(float)
478

1 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 2 -- Copyright (c) 2010-2018 by Enclustra GmbH, Switzerland 3 -- All rights reserved. 4 -- Authors: Martin Heimlicher, Oliver Bründler 5 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 6 7 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 8 -- Libraries 9 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 --! ieee library 11 library ieee; 12 --! logic package 13 use ieee.std_logic_1164.all; 14 --! package for conversions 15 use ieee.numeric_std.all; 16 --! package for real calculations 17 use ieee.math_real.all; 18 19 --! std library 20 library std; 21 --! used for file I/O 22 use std.textio.all; 23 24 25 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 26 -- Package Header 27 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 28 --! \brief Enclustra fix-point package 29 --! \details

Summary

30 --! The en_cl_fix package provides commonly used fixed-point arithmetic and vector resizing 31 --! operations implemented in VHDL and MATLAB code. The VHDL and MATLAB iimplementation deliver 32 --! bit-true results, simplifying bit-true co-simulation. 33 --! 34 --!

Formats

35 --! Format are always given in the form (S,I,F) where S defines if there is a sign bit, 36 --! I defines the number of integer bits and F defines the number of fractional bits.\n 37 --! en_cl_fix functions strictly operate on std_logic_vector (VHDL) or double (MATLAB)\n 38 --! Examples: 39 --!
    40 --!
  • (true,3,2) 6 bits, LSB = 0.25, min. = -8, max. = +7.75
    • 41 --!
  • (false,3,2) 5 bits, LSB = 0.25, min = 0, max. = +7.75
    • 42 --!
  • (false,-1,3) 2 bits, LSB = 0.125, min = 0, max = +0.375
    • 43 --!
  • (false,3,-1) 2 bits, LSB = 2, min = 0, max = 6
    • 44 --!
45 --! For further details see \link FixFormat_t \endlink. 46 --! 47 --!

Rounding

48 --! Most operations provide rounding and saturation support. See \link FixRound_t \endlink and \link FixSaturate_t \endlink 49 --! for details. 50 --! 51 --!

Related Packages

52 --! en_cl_bittrue_pkg: Used for data exchange between MATLAB and VHDL in bit-true testbenches \n 53 --! 54 --!

Examples

55 --! Variables are used in all examples but the syntax is exactly the same for signals.\n 56 --! All examples are implemented as processes but the statements can also be used as concurrent statements. 57 --! 58 --!

Addition

59 --! This example shows how a mathematical operation is implemented using the en_cl_fix library.\n 60 --! Results are printed to the console to be easily readable. 61 --! \verbatim 62 --! addition_p : process 63 --! -- Formats 64 --! constant OpAFmt_c : FixFormat_t := (true, 3, 5); 65 --! constant OpBFmt_c : FixFormat_t := (false, -2, 8); 66 --! constant ResFmt_c : FixFormat_t := (true, 3, 3); 67 --! 68 --! -- Variables 69 --! variable OpA_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(OpAFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 70 --! variable OpB_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(OpBFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 71 --! variable Res_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(ResFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 72 --! begin 73 --! OpA_v := cl_fix_from_real(-3.134, OpAFmt_c, Sat_s); 74 --! OpB_v := cl_fix_from_real(0.1, OpBFmt_c, Sat_s); 75 --! Res_v := cl_fix_add( OpA_v, OpAFmt_c, 76 --! OpB_v, OpBFmt_c, 77 --! ResFmt_c, NonSymPos_s, Sat_s); 78 --! report "Add: OpA is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(OpA_v, OpAFmt_c)); 79 --! report "Add: OpB is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(OpB_v, OpBFmt_c)); 80 --! report "Add: Res is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(Res_v, ResFmt_c)); 81 --! wait; 82 --! end process; 83 --! \endverbatim 84 --! 85 --!

Format Calculations

86 --! This example shows how formats can be calculated based on other formats. It implements a multiplication.\n 87 --! Results are printed to the console to be easily readable. 88 --! \verbatim 89 --! multiplication_p : process 90 --! -- Formats 91 --! constant OpAFmt_c : FixFormat_t := (true, 3, 5); 92 --! constant OpBFmt_c : FixFormat_t := (true, 2, 8); 93 --! constant ResFmt_c : FixFormat_t := ( OpAFmt_c.Signed or OpBFmt_c.Signed, 94 --! OpAFmt_c.IntBits + OpBFmt_c.IntBits + 1, 95 --! OpAFmt_c.FracBits + OpBFmt_c.FracBits); -- Calculate format for exact multiplication without rounding 96 --! 97 --! -- Variables 98 --! variable OpA_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(OpAFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 99 --! variable OpB_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(OpBFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 100 --! variable Res_v : std_logic_vector(cl_fix_width(ResFmt_c)-1 downto 0); 101 --! begin 102 --! OpA_v := cl_fix_from_real(-3.134, OpAFmt_c, Sat_s); 103 --! OpB_v := cl_fix_from_real(0.1, OpBFmt_c, Sat_s); 104 --! Res_v := cl_fix_mult( OpA_v, OpAFmt_c, 105 --! OpB_v, OpBFmt_c, 106 --! ResFmt_c, Trunc_s, None_s); 107 --! report "Mult: OpA is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(OpA_v, OpAFmt_c)); 108 --! report "Mult: OpB is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(OpB_v, OpBFmt_c)); 109 --! report "Mult: Res is " & real'image(cl_fix_to_real(Res_v, ResFmt_c)); 110 --! wait; 111 --! end process; 112 --! \endverbatim 113 package en_cl_fix_pkg is 114 115 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 116 -- Types 117 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 118 --! \brief Fix-point format type 119 --! 120 --! 121 --! 122 --! 123 --! 124 --!
Field Description Notes
Signed Signed/Unsigned -
IntBits Integer bits Can be negative, IntBits+FracBits must be at least 1
FracBits Fractional bits Can be negative, IntBits+FracBits must be at least 1
125 --! 126 --! unsigned examples (Signed = false) \n 127 --! \verbatim 128 --! III.FFF IntBits = 3 FracBits = 3 Width = 6 --> std_logic_vector (5 downto 0) 129 --! 0.FFF IntBits = 0 FracBits = 3 Width = 3 --> std_logic_vector (2 downto 0) 130 --! 0.00FFF IntBits = -2 FracBits = 5 Width = 3 --> std_logic_vector (2 downto 0) 131 --! III.000 IntBits = 3 FracBits = 0 Width = 3 --> std_logic_vector (2 downto 0) 132 --! III00.000 IntBits = 5 FracBits = -2 Width = 3 --> std_logic_vector (2 downto 0) 133 --! \endverbatim 134 --! 135 --! signed examples (Signed = true) \n 136 --! \verbatim 137 --! SIII.FFF IntBits = 3 FracBits = 3 Width = 7 --> std_logic_vector (6 downto 0) 138 --! S.FFF IntBits = 0 FracBits = 3 Width = 4 --> std_logic_vector (3 downto 0) 139 --! 0.0SFFF IntBits = -2 FracBits = 5 Width = 4 --> std_logic_vector (3 downto 0) 140 --! SIII.000 IntBits = 3 FracBits = 0 Width = 4 --> std_logic_vector (3 downto 0) 141 --! SIII00.000 IntBits = 5 FracBits = -2 Width = 4 --> std_logic_vector (3 downto 0) 142 --! \endverbatim 143 --! 144 --! \note All operations are performed with "infinite" precision and then rounded/saturated to 145 --! the result format. 146 --! 147 --! \note The sign bit is always the bit left to the most significant integer bit.\n 148 --! cl_fix_from_real (-16.0, cl_fix_format (true, 3, 0), None_s) --> "0000" (SIII) \n 149 --! The sign bit is zero, even if the number is negative. 150 --! This is because the "infinite" precision -16.0 is 151 --! "11111...111110000" and the last 4 bits are taken as the result. 152 --! 153 type FixFormat_t is record 154 Signed : boolean; 155 IntBits : integer; -- can be negative, IntBits+FracBits must be at least 1. 156 FracBits : integer; -- can be negative, IntBits+FracBits must be at least 1. 157 end record; 158 159 --! \brief Array of FixFormat_t 160 --! \see FixFormat_t 161 type FixFormatArray_t is array (natural range <>) of FixFormat_t; 162 163 --! \brief Rounding mode 164 --! 165 --! 166 --! 167 --! 168 --! 169 --! 170 --! 171 --! 172 --! 173 --! 174 --!
Value Description Examples rounded to (true,2,0)
2.2 2.7 -1.5 -0.5 0.5 1.5
Trunc_s Cut off bits without any rounding 2 2 -2 -1 0 1
NonSymPos_s Non-symmetric rounding to positive 2 3 -1 0 1 2
NonSymNeg_s Non-symmetric rounding to negative 2 3 -2 -1 0 1
SymInf_s Symmetric rounding to infinity 2 3 -2 -1 1 2
SymZero_s Symmetric rounding to zero 2 3 -1 0 0 1
ConvEven_s Convergent rounding to even numbers 2 3 -2 0 0 2
ConvOdd_s Convertent rounding to odd numbers 2 3 -1 -1 1 1
175 --! \note Use Trunc_S or NonSymPos_s for wherever possible for lowest resource usage 176 type FixRound_t is 177 ( 178 Trunc_s, -- cut off bits 179 NonSymPos_s, -- non-symmetric rounding to positive 180 NonSymNeg_s, -- non-symmetric rounding to negative 181 SymInf_s, -- symmetric rounding to infinity 182 SymZero_s, -- symmetric rounding to zero 183 ConvEven_s, -- convergent rounding to even number 184 ConvOdd_s -- convergent rounding to odd number 185 ); 186 --! \brief Alias for most common rounding mode 187 constant Round_s : FixRound_t := NonSymPos_s; 188 189 --! \brief Saturation mode 190 --! 191 --! 192 --! 193 --! 194 --! 195 --! 196 --!
Value Description
None_s No saturation, no warning
Warn_s No saturation, warning
Sat_s Saturation, no warning
SatWarn_s Saturation, warning
197 type FixSaturate_t is 198 ( 199 None_s, -- no saturation, no warning 200 Warn_s, -- no saturation, warning 201 Sat_s, -- saturation, no warning 202 SatWarn_s -- saturation, warning 203 ); 204 205 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 206 -- Format Functions 207 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 208 --! \brief Fills a FixFormat_t record with values (most useful in MATLAB) 209 --! \param signed Sign bit 210 --! \param intBits Integer bits 211 --! \param fracBits Fractional bits 212 --! \return Created FixFormat_t format 213 --! \see FixFormat_t 214 function cl_fix_format ( signed : boolean; 215 intBits : integer; 216 fracBits : integer) 217 return FixFormat_t; 218 219 220 --! \brief Get the number of bits to represent a fix-point format 221 --! \param fmt Format to get the number of bits required for 222 --! \return Number of bits required to represent the format 223 function cl_fix_width ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 224 return positive; 225 226 --! \brief Get the string representation of a fix-point format 227 --! \details The string is in the form "(true,3,4)"\n 228 --! This function is especially useful if the en_cl_bittrue_pkt package is used. 229 --! \param fmt Format to get the string representation for 230 --! \return String representation of the format 231 function cl_fix_string_from_format ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 232 return string; 233 234 --! \brief Converts a string into a FixFormat_t 235 --! \details This is very useful to pass formats as generics into simulations. Formats 236 --! cannot be passed as \link FixFormat_t \endlink since Modelsim only supports 237 --! Generics of types integer, string and boolean. Using this function, the formats 238 --! can be passed as string and then converted to \link FixFormat_t \endlink. 239 --! \param Str Format string in the form "(,,)" 240 --! \return FixFormat_t 241 function cl_fix_format_from_string( Str : string) 242 return FixFormat_t; 243 244 --! \brief Zero in a given format 245 --! \param fmt Format to represent zero in 246 --! \return Fix-point representation of zero 247 --! \see cl_fix_max_value() 248 --! \see cl_fix_min_value() 249 function cl_fix_zero_value ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 250 return std_logic_vector; 251 252 --! \brief Maximum value in a given format 253 --! \param fmt Format to get the maximum value from 254 --! \return Fix-point representation of the maximum value representable in format fmt 255 --! \see cl_fix_zero_value() 256 --! \see cl_fix_min_value() 257 function cl_fix_max_value ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 258 return std_logic_vector; 259 260 --! \brief Minimum value in a given format 261 --! \param fmt Format to get the minimum value from 262 --! \return Fix-point representation of the minimum value representable in format fmt 263 --! \see cl_fix_zero_value() 264 --! \see cl_fix_max_value() 265 function cl_fix_min_value ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 266 return std_logic_vector; 267 268 --! \brief Maximum value in a given format as real number 269 --! \param fmt Format to get the maximum value from 270 --! \return Real representation of the maximum value representable in format fmt 271 --! \see cl_fix_min_real() 272 function cl_fix_max_real ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 273 return real; 274 275 --! \brief Minimum value in a given format as real number 276 --! \param fmt Format to get the minimum value from 277 --! \return Real representation of the minimum value representable in format fmt 278 --! \see cl_fix_max_real() 279 function cl_fix_min_real ( fmt : FixFormat_t) 280 return real; 281 282 --! \brief Extract sign bit from a fix-point number 283 --! \param a Number to extract sign bit from 284 --! \param a_fmt Format of a 285 --! \return Extracted sign bit 286 --! \see cl_fix_int() 287 --! \see cl_fix_frac() 288 function cl_fix_sign ( a : std_logic_vector; 289 a_fmt : FixFormat_t) 290 return std_logic; 291 292 --! \brief Extract integer bits from fix-point number 293 --! \param a Number to extract integer bits from 294 --! \param a_fmt Format of a 295 --! \return Extracted integer bits 296 --! \see cl_fix_sign() 297 --! \see cl_fix_frac() 298 function cl_fix_int ( a : std_logic_vector; 299 a_fmt : FixFormat_t) 300 return std_logic_vector; 301 302 --! \brief Extract fractional bits from fix-point number 303 --! \param a Number to extract integer bits from 304 --! \param a_fmt Format of a 305 --! \see cl_fix_sign() 306 --! \see cl_fix_int() 307 function cl_fix_frac ( a : std_logic_vector; 308 a_fmt : FixFormat_t) 309 return std_logic_vector; 310 311 --! \brief Combine sign bit, integer bits and fractional bits to a fix-point number 312 --! \param sign Sign bit 313 --! \param int Integer bits 314 --! \param frac Fractional bits 315 --! \param result_fmt Format of the returned fix-point number 316 --! \return Combined fix-point number 317 function cl_fix_combine ( sign : std_logic; 318 int : std_logic_vector; 319 frac : std_logic_vector; 320 result_fmt : FixFormat_t) 321 return std_logic_vector; 322 323 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 324 -- Bit Manipulation 325 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 326 --! 1690 constant ChunkSize_c : positive := 30; 1691 constant ChunkCount_c : positive := (ABits_c + ChunkSize_c - 1)/ChunkSize_c; 1692 variable a_v : std_logic_vector(a'length-1 downto 0); 1693 variable Correction_v : real := 0.0; 1694 variable apad_v : unsigned(ChunkSize_c*ChunkCount_c-1 downto 0); 1695 variable Chunk_v : unsigned(ChunkSize_c-1 downto 0); 1696 variable result_v : real := 0.0; 1697 begin 1698 -- Enforce 'downto' bit order 1699 a_v := a; 1700 1701 -- Handle sign bit 1702 if a_fmt.Signed and a_v(ABits_c-1) = '1' then 1703 a_v(ABits_c-1) := '0'; -- Clear sign bit. 1704 Correction_v := -2.0**(ABits_c-1 - a_fmt.FracBits); -- Remember its weight. 1705 end if; 1706 1707 -- Resize to an integer number of chunks 1708 apad_v := resize(unsigned(a_v), ChunkSize_c*ChunkCount_c); 1709 1710 -- Convert to real in chunks 1711 for i in ChunkCount_c-1 downto 0 loop 1712 result_v := result_v * 2.0**ChunkSize_c; -- Shift to next chunk. 1713 Chunk_v := apad_v((i+1)*ChunkSize_c-1 downto i*ChunkSize_c); 1714 result_v := result_v + real(to_integer(Chunk_v)) * 2.0**(-a_fmt.FracBits); 1715 end loop; 1716 1717 -- Add sign bit contribution 1718 result_v := result_v + Correction_v; 1719 1720 return result_v; 1721 end; 1722 1723 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1724 --! cl_fix_from_bin implementation 1725 function cl_fix_from_bin ( a : string; 1726 result_fmt : FixFormat_t) 1727 return std_logic_vector is 1728 variable a_v : string (1 to a'length); 1729 variable result_v : std_logic_vector (a'length-1 downto 0); 1730 variable pos_v : natural; 1731 begin 1732 a_v := a; 1733 pos_v := a'length; 1734 for i in 1 to a'length loop 1735 case a_v (i) is 1736 when '0' => 1737 pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1738 result_v (pos_v) := '0'; 1739 when '1' => 1740 pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1741 result_v (pos_v) := '1'; 1742 when 'b' | 'B' => 1743 if i = 2 and a_v (1) = '0' then 1744 pos_v := a'length; 1745 end if; 1746 when '_' => 1747 when others => 1748 report "cl_fix_from_bin : Illegal character in binary string!" 1749 severity error; 1750 end case; 1751 end loop; 1752 assert a'length-pos_v = cl_fix_width (result_fmt); 1753 report "cl_fix_from_bin : The binary string doesn't have the correct length!" 1754 severity error; 1755 return result_v (a'length-1 downto pos_v); 1756 end; 1757 1758 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1759 --! cl_fix_to_bin implementation 1760 function cl_fix_to_bin ( a : std_logic_vector; 1761 a_fmt : FixFormat_t) 1762 return string is 1763 begin 1764 return toString(a); 1765 end; 1766 1767 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1768 --! cl_fix_from_hex implementation 1769 function cl_fix_from_hex ( a : string; 1770 result_fmt : FixFormat_t) 1771 return std_logic_vector is 1772 constant ResultWidth_c : positive := cl_fix_width (result_fmt); 1773 variable a_v : string (1 to a'length); 1774 variable result_v : std_logic_vector (a'length*4-1 downto 0); 1775 variable pos_v : natural; 1776 begin 1777 a_v := a; 1778 pos_v := a'length; 1779 for i in 1 to a_v'length loop 1780 case a_v (i) is 1781 when '0' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0000"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1782 when '1' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0001"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1783 when '2' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0010"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1784 when '3' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0011"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1785 when '4' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0100"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1786 when '5' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0101"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1787 when '6' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0110"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1788 when '7' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "0111"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1789 when '8' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1000"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1790 when '9' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1001"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1791 when 'a' | 'A' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1010"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1792 when 'b' | 'B' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1011"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1793 when 'c' | 'C' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1100"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1794 when 'd' | 'D' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1101"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1795 when 'e' | 'E' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1110"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1796 when 'f' | 'F' => result_v (pos_v*4+3 downto pos_v*4) := "1111"; pos_v := pos_v - 1; 1797 when 'x' | 'X' => 1798 if i = 2 and a_v (1) = '0' then 1799 pos_v := a'length; 1800 end if; 1801 when '_' => 1802 when others => 1803 report "cl_fix_from_hex : Illegal character in hexadecimal string!" 1804 severity error; 1805 end case; 1806 end loop; 1807 assert 4*(a'length-pos_v) >= ResultWidth_c and 4*(a'length-pos_v-1) < ResultWidth_c; 1808 report "cl_fix_from_hex : The hexadecimal string doesn't have the correct length!" 1809 severity error; 1810 if ResultWidth_c/4*4 < ResultWidth_c then 1811 assert unsigned (result_v (a'length*4-1 downto pos_v*4+ResultWidth_c)) = 0 1812 report "cl_fix_from_hex : The unused bits in the hexadecimal string are not all equal to zero!" 1813 severity error; 1814 end if; 1815 return result_v (pos_v*4+ResultWidth_c-1 downto pos_v*4); 1816 end; 1817 1818 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1819 --! cl_fix_to_hex implementation 1820 function cl_fix_to_hex ( a : std_logic_vector; 1821 a_fmt : FixFormat_t) 1822 return string is 1823 begin 1824 return toHexString (a);
